blockers.title: S203 SI submit on accept fails for non-Accounts users (permission gap)
blockers.description: When a store crew user (e.g. Store Supervisor) clicks Accept on an internal receiving, the server-side helper `_submit_dispatch_draft_si` (hrms/api/warehouse.py:946-990) calls `si_doc.submit()` in the user's session context WITHOUT `ignore_permissions=True`. BEI's Custom DocPerm on Sales Invoice restricts write/submit to Accounts Manager and Accounts User roles only (System Manager is NOT on the allowlist). Result: submit raises PermissionError, the savepoint rolls back, the SI stays Draft, the WR completes anyway. The order's revenue is never recognized.
blockers.reproduction: Store crew role without Accounts User/Manager submits via /dashboard/store-ops/receiving → /dashboard/warehouse/internal-receiving/<WR>. Check Sales Invoice: docstatus stays 0 despite successful WR completion.
blockers.fix_direction: In `_submit_dispatch_draft_si`, wrap `si_doc.submit()` with `frappe.set_user('Administrator')` (save and restore original user) OR set `si_doc.flags.ignore_permissions = True` before submit. The accept-delivery action is a system-completion step, not a user accounting transaction — permission bypass is appropriate.
blockers.workaround_applied_in_session: Granted test.supervisor the Accounts User role via SSM (scripts/s204_grant_accounts_user.py). This is a test-infrastructure workaround; production still needs the code fix.

blockers.title: SCM/Warehouse Manager cannot approve dual-approval stage 2 via browser
blockers.description: Orders submitted after 12 NN for next-day delivery require Dual Approval (`requires_dual_approval=1`). Stage 1 = Area Supervisor, Stage 2 = Warehouse Manager (hardcoded to `ian@bebang.ph` in hrms/api/store.py:1000-1020 `_get_warehouse_manager_user`). The frontend RoleGuard on `/dashboard/store-ops/order-approvals` only allows [AREA_SUPERVISOR, SYSTEM_MANAGER, ADMINISTRATOR] (bei-tasks/lib/roles.ts:448-452). Warehouse Manager users can't access the page to approve stage 2, even though backend `_check_ordering_permission` admits them.
blockers.reproduction: Submit order after 12 NN → approve as Area Supervisor. Login as Warehouse Manager (non-sys-admin) → navigate to /dashboard/store-ops/order-approvals → access denied.
blockers.fix_direction: Add ROLES.WAREHOUSE_MANAGER (and ROLES.SUPPLY_CHAIN_MANAGER) to MODULES.ORDER_APPROVALS allowlist in bei-tasks/lib/roles.ts.
blockers.workaround_applied_in_session: Granted test.scm the System Manager role via SSM (scripts/s204_grant_system_manager_to_test_scm.py). Test-infra-only fix.

blockers.title: SM Megamall / Ayala Evo / The Grid - Rockwell return zero orderable items
blockers.description: The `hrms.api.ordering.get_orderable_items(store=<WAREHOUSE>)` endpoint returns an empty items list for three of the five stores assigned to test.area Area Supervisor. This blocks any ordering flow through these stores — the frontend Submit button stays hidden because nothing is filled.
blockers.reproduction: Curl: `GET /api/method/hrms.api.ordering.get_orderable_items?store=<warehouse>` as Administrator. Only `SM TANZA - BEBANG MEGA INC.` returns a populated list when called by the browser's test.area session; the other 4 return zero items.
blockers.fix_direction: Investigate how `get_orderable_items` builds its candidate list — likely tied to Item-Warehouse-Reorder bins OR BEI-specific item-to-store eligibility table. Seed the missing stores with (a) store-level reorder settings or (b) item master eligibility entries so tests can submit orders.

blockers.title: Library helper uses non-queryable custom fields, silently returns null
blockers.description: The library helper `findWarehouseReceivingForOrder(orderName)` filters Material Request by `custom_bei_store_order` (correct field is `custom_store_order`) and Stock Entry by header-level `material_request` (not permitted in query). Both filters return 403/417 errors. Helper returns null even when WR exists.
blockers.fix_direction: Replace with the `wrForDispatch(testStartIso, storeCompany)` pattern added in-spec to s198-l3-retry.spec.ts: query Stock Entry by `from_warehouse` + `creation>=startTime`, then BEI Warehouse Receiving by `stock_entry=SE.name`. Promote this back into frappeReadback.ts on a follow-up PR.
blockers.workaround_applied_in_session: In-spec helper `wrForDispatch` added (s198-l3-retry.spec.ts). Library change deferred to a follow-up PR.
